title: make test requires Python 3.9 - a minor tweak for 3.8
body:
Trying the latest Verilator built from source, my PY version is:

> Python 3.8.12 (default, Mar 15 2025, 23:53:23) 

Throws error as:
```log
  File "./driver.py", line 55, in <module>
    Arg_Tests: list[str] = []
TypeError: 'type' object is not subscriptable
make: *** [smoke-test] Error 1
```
Fix is really simple - in driver.py I had to add:
```python
 from __future__ import annotations
```

Not sure if the developers want to fix that, updating here for the sake of wider community if they face similar error.

Thanks
code_blocks:
File "./driver.py", line 55, in <module>
    Arg_Tests: list[str] = []
TypeError: 'type' object is not subscriptable
make: *** [smoke-test] Error 1
 from __future__ import annotations
